title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: Full test suite: Single test file context: Generate documentation roxygen: Full package check (runs R CMD CHECK equivalent): Code formatting: Uses air formatter configured air.toml (100 char line width, 4-space indent). Run edits air format . main directory.
code: devtools::test() devtools::test(filter = "schema")     # test-schema.R devtools::test(filter = "validate")   # test-validate.R devtools::document() devtools::check()

title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: schmear developer-focused package creating structured data frame subtypes. three core layers:

title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: sch_schema(...) – Top-level function defines structured data frame schema. Takes named column specifications (type constructors) optional description. Type Constructors – sch_numeric(), sch_integer(), sch_character(), sch_factor(), sch_date(), sch_datetime(), sch_logical(), sch_inherits(), sch_list_of(), sch_custom(). Type registry – type_fns list maps type names check/msg/coerce functions. Custom types added way. Coercion convention – type_fns[[type]]$coerce(x, type) always accepts value full type spec. Wrap base coercers like .Date() / .POSIXct() second argument misinterpreted.

title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: sch_validate(schema, data, check=c("names","types","distinct","relationships"), ...) – Core validation function. "names" → validate_names() – missing required columns, extra columns, missing inner columns nests "types" → validate_types_missing() – type mismatches, NAs missing=FALSE "distinct" → validate_distinct() – duplicate values distinct=TRUE columns (NAs excluded per semantics) "relationships" → validate_relationships() – key uniqueness, crossing completeness, nesting completeness Error handling – Collects issues raising single sch_validation_error attached $issues list. Nested schemas – Validation recurses nested data-frame element tracks nested path plus element index issues.

title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: print.sch_schema() – Pretty-prints schema indentation nesting levels, type descriptions, constraint flags. format.sch_schema() – Returns named character vector internal consumption.

title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: Every type type_fns structure: Important: Check functions must wrap non-logical results isTRUE() validation avoid crashes NA returns.
code: type_name = list(     check = function(x, type) { ... },      # Returns TRUE/FALSE; must handle NA gracefully     msg = function(type) { ... },           # Returns human-readable type description     coerce = function(x, type) { ... }      # Attempts coercion (may fail) )

title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: Regular columns – NAs explicitly allowed/forbidden per missing attribute. List columns – NULL list elements treated “missing” (analogous NA). Distinct checking – NAs/NULLs excluded uniqueness checks missing=TRUE (“duplicate NA” error). Custom checks – Must handle NAs gracefully; check returns NA, ’s treated type failure, crash.

title: Copilot Instructions for schmear
text: sch_validate() modularization – check phase can run independently. vctrs integration – Uses vctrs::vec_unique_count(), vec_group_loc(), vec_slice() vectorized operations; handles NA semantics correctly. Error collection – validators return list issues (possibly empty); issues collected, printed bullet list cli::cli_abort(). Tests – Prefer testthat::test_that() expect_* assertions, matching existing test-schema.R test-validate.R style.

title: Build Structured Data Frame Subtypes
text: Provides developer-focused helper functions S3 classes ease creation structured subtypes data frames require certain columns types present, preserve special attributes common data frame manipulations.

title: Build Structured Data Frame Subtypes
text: can install development version schmear GitHub :

title: dplyr integration for sch_df — dplyr-sch_df
text: methods hook dplyr's three extension generics (dplyr::dplyr_row_slice(), dplyr::dplyr_col_modify(), dplyr::dplyr_reconstruct()) plus base-R names() replacement 1-d [ enforce schema constraints across dplyr operations near-zero overhead.

title: dplyr integration for sch_df — dplyr-sch_df
text: method calls NextMethod() first (performing actual dplyr operation) re-validates result schema, running checks can plausibly violated type operation:

what: Row slicing (arrange, filter, slice, semi/anti joins)
title: dplyr integration for sch_df — dplyr-sch_df
text: Row operations introduce new name type violations, validation run default. Relationship constraints (crossing completeness, primary-key uniqueness) can broken removing rows. Pass .check_relationships = TRUE ... opt check.

title: dplyr integration for sch_df — dplyr-sch_df
text: column modification can delete required columns (via NULL assignment), assign wrong type, introduce duplicate values distinct = TRUE column, three cheap checks run.

title: dplyr integration for sch_df — dplyr-sch_df
text: dplyr_reconstruct() called joins. names types checked: distinct relationship checks omitted joins can intentionally produce non-distinct rows incomplete crossings.

title: dplyr integration for sch_df — dplyr-sch_df
text: 1-d [ call selects reorders columns. Reordering always safe; selecting column subset drop required columns, names check run.

title: dplyr integration for sch_df — dplyr-sch_df
text: Renaming column belongs schema (either directly member sch_multiple() group) never valid without also updating schema definition, error raised immediately.

what: Coerce a data frame to conform to a schema — sch_coerce
title: Coerce a data frame to conform to a schema — sch_coerce
text: Attempts coerce column data type expected schema, using coercion method defined column type. coercion, optionally validates result sch_validate().

title: Coerce a data frame to conform to a schema — sch_coerce
code: sch_coerce(schema, data, validate = TRUE, call = rlang::caller_env())

title: Coerce a data frame to conform to a schema — sch_coerce
text: schema schema object created sch_schema(). data data frame coerce. validate TRUE (default), sch_validate() called coerced data columns processed. Set FALSE skip validation, can useful want inspect coerced result checking constraints. call environment call used error reporting, passed rlang::abort(). Useful wrapping sch_coerce() inside another function errors point right place.

title: Coerce a data frame to conform to a schema — sch_coerce
text: data columns coerced schema types, invisibly, coercion succeeds. column coerced, error class sch_coercion_error raised summary failures. validate = TRUE, subsequent sch_validate() call may also raise sch_validation_error coerced data still violates schema constraints (e.g., --bounds values uniqueness violations).

title: Coerce a data frame to conform to a schema — sch_coerce
text: Coercion applied column--column using coerce function registered type internal type_fns registry. example, column specified sch_integer() coerced .integer(). Nested schemas (created sch_nest()) handled recursing element data frame, grouped columns (created sch_multiple()) member column coerced individually. Columns present data named schema (.e., covered sch_others()) left untouched.

title: Coerce a data frame to conform to a schema — sch_coerce
code: schema <- sch_schema(     id = sch_integer(distinct = TRUE),     name = sch_character(missing = FALSE),     score = sch_numeric() )  # Coerce a data frame with character columns df <- data.frame(id = c("1", "2", "3"), name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"), score = 1:3) str(sch_coerce(schema, df)) #> 'data.frame':	3 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ id   : int  1 2 3 #>  $ name : chr  "Alice" "Bob" "Carol" #>  $ score: num  1 2 3  # Nested schema coercion nested_schema <- sch_schema(     group = sch_factor(),     info = sch_nest(x = sch_numeric(), y = sch_integer()) ) nested_df <- data.frame(group = "A") nested_df$info <- list(data.frame(x = "1.5", y = "2")) str(sch_coerce(nested_schema, nested_df)) #> 'data.frame':	1 obs. of  2 variables: #>  $ group: Factor w/ 1 level "A": 1 #>  $ info :List of 1 #>   ..$ :'data.frame':	1 obs. of  2 variables: #>   .. ..$ x: num 1.5 #>   .. ..$ y: int 2

what: Construct and validate a schema-aware data frame — sch_df
title: Construct and validate a schema-aware data frame — sch_df
text: Bare-bones constructor data frame attached schema. function called package developers writing internal constructors. checks types data schema. validate_sch_df() lightweight wrapper around sch_validate() also returns input.

title: Construct and validate a schema-aware data frame — sch_df
code: new_sch_df(data, schema, groups = NULL, class = NULL, use_tbl = TRUE)  validate_sch_df(x)

title: Construct and validate a schema-aware data frame — sch_df
text: data data frame. schema sch_schema object. groups named list character vectors column names, use sch_multiple(). class Additional classes add object, addition "sch_df" tibble/data.frame classes. use_tbl TRUE, returned object tibble classes tbl_df tbl addition data.frame. reminder, tbl_df affects behavior object (slicing, row names, etc.), tbl affects printing . x sch_df object.

title: Construct and validate a schema-aware data frame — sch_df
text: data frame class c(class, "sch_df", ...) attributes sch_schema sch_groups. input, validation successful.

title: Construct and validate a schema-aware data frame — sch_df
code: schema = sch_schema(     .desc = "MCMC draws",     .relationships = ~ chain * draw * parameter,     chain = sch_integer("Chain number"),     draw = sch_integer("Draw number", bounds = c(1, Inf), closed = c(TRUE, FALSE)),     parameter = sch_character("Parameter name"),     value = sch_numeric("Parameter value") ) d_raw = data.frame(chain = 1L, draw = 1:4, parameter = "mu", value = rnorm(4)) d = new_sch_df(d_raw, schema, class="mcmc_draws") validate_sch_df(d) #> # MCMC draws: 4 column rules #> # A tibble:   4 × 4 #>   chain  draw parameter    value #>   <int> <int> <chr>        <dbl> #> 1     1     1 mu        -1.40    #> 2     1     2 mu         0.255   #> 3     1     3 mu        -2.44    #> 4     1     4 mu        -0.00557 str(d) #> mcmc_drw [4 × 4] (S3: mcmc_draws/sch_df/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ chain    : int [1:4] 1 1 1 1 #>  $ draw     : int [1:4] 1 2 3 4 #>  $ parameter: chr [1:4] "mu" "mu" "mu" "mu" #>  $ value    : num [1:4] -1.40004 0.25532 -2.43726 -0.00557 #>  - attr(*, "sch_schema")=List of 2 #>   ..$ cols         :List of 4 #>   .. ..$ chain    :List of 3 #>   .. .. ..$ type  : chr "integer" #>   .. .. ..$ bounds: num [1:2] -Inf Inf #>   .. .. ..$ closed: logi [1:2] TRUE TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "desc")= chr "Chain number" #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "missing")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "required")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "distinct")= logi FALSE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "sch_type" #>   .. ..$ draw     :List of 3 #>   .. .. ..$ type  : chr "integer" #>   .. .. ..$ bounds: num [1:2] 1 Inf #>   .. .. ..$ closed: logi [1:2] TRUE FALSE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "desc")= chr "Draw number" #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "missing")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "required")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "distinct")= logi FALSE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "sch_type" #>   .. ..$ parameter:List of 1 #>   .. .. ..$ type: chr "character" #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "desc")= chr "Parameter name" #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "missing")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "required")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "distinct")= logi FALSE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "sch_type" #>   .. ..$ value    :List of 3 #>   .. .. ..$ type  : chr "numeric" #>   .. .. ..$ bounds: num [1:2] -Inf Inf #>   .. .. ..$ closed: logi [1:2] TRUE TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "desc")= chr "Parameter value" #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "missing")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "required")= logi TRUE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "distinct")= logi FALSE #>   .. .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "sch_type" #>   ..$ relationships:List of 2 #>   .. ..$ type    : chr "cross" #>   .. ..$ children:List of 3 #>   .. .. ..$ :List of 2 #>   .. .. .. ..$ type: chr "var" #>   .. .. .. ..$ name: chr "chain" #>   .. .. ..$ :List of 2 #>   .. .. .. ..$ type: chr "var" #>   .. .. .. ..$ name: chr "draw" #>   .. .. ..$ :List of 2 #>   .. .. .. ..$ type: chr "var" #>   .. .. .. ..$ name: chr "parameter" #>   ..- attr(*, "desc")= chr "MCMC draws" #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "sch_schema"

what: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: Defines structure single 'observation' structured data frame. column type restrictions may required optional. Schemas support nesting relationships.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(..., .desc = NULL, .relationships = NULL)  sch_others()  sch_any(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_multiple(   name,   type,   desc = NULL,   required = TRUE,   check = NULL,   msg = NULL,   coerce = NULL )  sch_nest(..., .desc = NULL)  sch_numeric(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_integer(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_logical(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_character(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_factor(   desc = NULL,   levels = NULL,   strict = TRUE,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_date(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.Date(-Inf), as.Date(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_datetime(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.POSIXct(-Inf), as.POSIXct(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_inherits(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_list_of(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_custom(   name,   desc = NULL,   check,   msg,   coerce,   ...,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: ... Column specifications, form col_name = col_type pairs, col_type call column type constructor listed , sch_numeric(). Every type must kind vector, .e., vctrs::obj_is_vector() must return TRUE. columns must named, except sch_others(), described , sch_multiple(), describes group columns sharing type. named sch_nest() describes columns stored nested data frame. special function sch_others() indicates preferred location columns explicitly mentioned schema. sch_others() appears, columns allowed. Trailing commas permitted. .relationships optional one-sided formula describing structural relationships values different columns. Formulas can involve named arguments .... Use * signify crossed levels, verify combinations exist, / signify nested levels, + create compound keys (bundling columns single identifier). See examples . desc, .desc description column consumers schema. type contraints described separately need included description.  example "age", description might "Age patient years", "Non-negative integer representing age patient years". overall sch_schema, desc printed part header data frames implementing schema, default. missing TRUE, column may contain missing values. Otherwise, missing values result error. required TRUE (default), group entry sch_groups must contain least one column name. FALSE, empty character vector entry also accepted. distinct TRUE, column must contain duplicate values (accounting nesting structure). name name custom type. type column type constructor (e.g. sch_numeric()) specifying expected type every column group. check two-argument function checks whether object satisfies type. first argument object check, second full type specification. msg one-argument function generates descriptive message type passed type object . end period. coerce two-argument function attempts coerce object type. first argument object coerce, second full type specification. bounds Length-two vector c(min, max) specifying allowed range values. closed Length-two logical vector specifying whether bounds closed (inclusive) open (exclusive). levels character vector factor levels, NULL enforce specific levels. strict TRUE, factors specified levels accepted. FALSE, character vectors specified levels also accepted. class character vector class names.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: object class sch_schema,

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: sch_others(): placeholder non-required columns schema. sch_any(): column type. type checking performed. sch_multiple(): group multiple columns sharing type. group identified name, must appear entry sch_groups attribute data frame validated. entry character vector column names belong group. Optionally accepts cross-column check, msg, coerce functions applied entire group per-column type checks pass. must provided together . sch_multiple() must unnamed sch_schema() call. Per-column constraints missing distinct set inner type argument. sch_nest(): set columns stored nested list-column data frames. Must given name outer sch_schema(). sch_numeric(): numeric vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_integer(): integer vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_logical(): logical vector. sch_character(): character vector. sch_factor(): factor specified levels. sch_date(): Date vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_datetime(): POSIXct vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_inherits(): list-column whose elements satisfy inherits(_, class). sch_list_of(): vector satisfying inherits(_, class). sch_custom(): custom type defined user-provided check, type message, coercion functions. Additional named values stored along type specification may passed via ... available check, message, coercion function elements type argument.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(     .desc = "MCMC draws",     .relationships = ~ chain * draw * parameter,     chain = sch_integer("Chain number"),     draw = sch_integer("Draw number", bounds = c(1, Inf), closed = c(TRUE, FALSE)),     parameter = sch_factor("Parameter name", levels = c("mu", "sigma", "log_lik")),     value = sch_numeric("Parameter value") ) #> MCMC draws #> A schema with 4 required elements: #>     chain  Chain number: An integer vector. #>      draw  Draw number: An integer vector with values in [1, Inf). #> parameter  Parameter name: A factor; one of mu, sigma, or log_lik. #>     value  Parameter value: A numeric vector. #> Relationships: chain × draw × parameter  sch_schema(     .desc = "Student data",     .relationships = ~ (grade + teacher) / table_group,     birthday = sch_date("Date of birth", required = FALSE),     height = sch_numeric(         "Height in inches",         bounds = c(0, 108),         closed = c(FALSE, TRUE)     ),     grade = sch_factor(strict = FALSE, levels = c("Kindergarten", "1st", "2nd")),     teacher = sch_nest(         first = sch_character("First name"),         last = sch_character("Last name")     ),     table_group = sch_integer(bounds=c(1, 6)),     enrolled = sch_logical(missing = FALSE),     sch_others() ) #> Student data #> A schema with 5 required elements: #>    birthday  (optional) Date of birth: A date vector. #>      height  Height in inches: A numeric vector with values in (0, 108]. #>       grade  A factor or character; one of Kindergarten, 1st, or 2nd. #>     teacher  (nested): #>              first  First name: A character vector. #>               last  Last name: A character vector. #> table_group  An integer vector with values in [1, 6]. #>    enrolled  A logical vector. No NAs allowed. #>         ...  Other columns #> Relationships: (grade + teacher) / table_group  sch_schema(     .desc = "Causal inference data",     treatment = sch_factor(levels = c("control", "treatment"), missing = FALSE),     outcome = sch_numeric(missing = FALSE),     sch_multiple("covariates", type = sch_any(missing = FALSE), required = FALSE),     sch_others() ) #> Causal inference data #> A schema with 2 required elements: #>  treatment  A factor; one of control or treatment. No NAs allowed. #>    outcome  A numeric vector. No NAs allowed. #> covariates  (multiple; optional): each a vector of any type. No NAs allowed. #>        ...  Other columns  sch_custom(    name = "even",    check = function(x, type) is.integer(x) && all(x %% 2 == 0),    msg = function(type) "vector of even integers",    coerce = function(x, type) (as.integer(x) %/% 2) * 2 ) #> A vector of even integers

what: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: Checks data frame conforms schema, validating column presence, types, missing values, uniqueness constraints, nesting structure.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: sch_validate(   schema,   data,   check = c("names", "types", "distinct", "relationships"),   call = rlang::caller_env() )

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: schema schema object created sch_schema(). data data frame validate. check character vector specifying checks perform. default runs checks. Possible values: "names": check missing required columns unexpected extra columns. "types": check column types missing-value (NA) constraints. "distinct": check uniqueness constraints columns marked distinct = TRUE. Relatively expensive. "relationships": validate relationship formulas (primary-key uniqueness crossing/nesting completeness). Relatively expensive. call environment call used error reporting, passed rlang::abort(). Useful wrapping sch_validate() inside another function error points right place.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: data, invisibly, validation succeeds. Otherwise, error class sch_validation_error raised formatted summary issues found.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: # Basic validation: valid data passes silently schema <- sch_schema(     id = sch_integer(distinct = TRUE),     name = sch_character(missing = FALSE),     age = sch_numeric(required = FALSE) ) df <- data.frame(id = 1:3, name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"), age = c(25, NA, 30)) sch_validate(schema, df)  # Invalid, throw validation errors if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # missing required columns sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = 1:2))  # type constrains not satisfied sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = c(1L, 1L), name = c("Alice", NA))) } # }
